title: Metabolite Production and Extraction of Indole Compound From Tomato Endophyte Streptomyces sp.
author: Veilumuthu Pattapulavar
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Yeast malt agar - ISP2 agar (see Recipes)
Streptomyces DNA Purification kit (Himedia, CAS number: MBA
Mueller Hinton agar (MHA) (Himedia, CAS number: M
Utilize ARTS for antibiotic-specific genome mining. Table

steps.title: Healthy 4-week-old tomato plants grown in...
steps.instruction: Healthy 4-week-old tomato plants grown in natural soil under conditions ( Lycopersicon esculentum ) (Arka Rakshak)

steps.title: International Streptomyces Project -2 agar (Himedia, CAS number: 8002-48-
steps.instruction: International Streptomyces Project -2 agar (Himedia, CAS number: 8002-48-

steps.title: Yeast malt agar - ISP2 agar...
steps.instruction: Yeast malt agar - ISP2 agar Ingredients Final concentration Peptone 5 g/L Yeast extract 3 g/L Malt extract 3 g/L Dextrose (glucose) 10 g/L Agar 20 g/L

steps.title: MHA agar Ingredients Final concentration HM...
steps.instruction: MHA agar Ingredients Final concentration HM infusion solids B # (from 300 g) 2 g/L Acicase 17.5 g/L Starch 1.5 g/L Agar

steps.title: Collect healthy tomato plants ( Lycopersicon esculentum )
steps.instruction: Collect healthy tomato plants ( Lycopersicon esculentum ). In our work, healthy 4-week-old tomato plants (L. esculentum) , from Madurai (9.9420° N, 77.9724° E), India, grown in natural soil in an agricultural field (37 ± 2 °C, 12 h light/dark photoperiod), were used for endophyte isolation (lower stem 3–5 cm, plant measured upward from the soil surface).

steps.title: Perform surface sterilization of plant samples...
steps.instruction: Perform surface sterilization of plant samples using stems collected from a 4-week-old tomato plant. Cut the stems into 4–5 cm long segments using sterile surgical blades and do the following: a. Immerse in 70% ethanol for 1 min. b. Immerse in 90% ethanol for 1 min. c. Treat with 0.9% sodium hypochlorite for 4 min. d. Rinse in 70% ethanol for 30 s. e. Soak in 10% NaHCO 3 for 5 min. f. Rinse thoroughly three times with sterile distilled water.

steps.title: Cut the surface-sterilized lower stems in...
steps.instruction: Cut the surface-sterilized lower stems in a cross direction and streak on ISP2 agar (pH 7.2 before autoclaving at 121 °C, 15 psi for 15 min) medium supplemented with cyclohexamide and nystatin (each used at 50 μg/mL in ISP2 agar). Stock solutions (10 mg/mL) were prepared in ethanol and filter-sterilized before use.

steps.title: Then, incubate the plates at 30...
steps.instruction: Then, incubate the plates at 30 °C for 15 days and isolate individual microbial colonies. All incubation steps should be carried out in a light/dark cycle at 30 °C to support endophyte growth. From this point onward, all procedures should be conducted under a clean bench/laminar flow hood to maintain sterility

steps.title: After 15 days, pick distinct colonies...
steps.instruction: After 15 days, pick distinct colonies with a powdery morphology and subculture onto fresh ISP2 agar plates to ensure purity and morphological consistency ( Figure 1 ). Figure

steps.title: Isolation of endophytic Streptomyces sp
steps.instruction: Isolation of endophytic Streptomyces sp. VITGV100 B. Whole-genome sequencing

steps.title: Grow subcultures in ISP2 broth at...
steps.instruction: Grow subcultures in ISP2 broth at 30 °C for 7 days with shaking at 120 rpm.

steps.title: Extract genomic DNA using the CTAB method (Veilumuthu et al
steps.instruction: Extract genomic DNA using the CTAB method (Veilumuthu et al., 2022).

steps.title: Assess DNA quality and quantity using NanoDrop
steps.instruction: Assess DNA quality and quantity using NanoDrop. Typical DNA concentration is 146.9 ng/μL.

steps.title: Prepare paired-end sequencing libraries using Illumina...
steps.instruction: Prepare paired-end sequencing libraries using Illumina TruSeq Nano DNA Library Prep kit. In our case, paired-end sequencing was performed with 2 × 150 bp reads, achieving ~100× coverage. (Genome sequence deposited in NCBI; SRA accession number SRS9635924.)

steps.title: Process sequencing data using Trimmomatic v0
steps.instruction: Process sequencing data using Trimmomatic v0.38 to obtain high-quality reads. Perform quality trimming of raw reads using Trimmomatic v0.38 to remove adapter sequences and low-quality regions. Recommended command line (paired-end data): https://github.com/usadellab/Trimmomatic .

steps.title: Assemble genome using SPAdes assembler v3
steps.instruction: Assemble genome using SPAdes assembler v3.13.

steps.title: Assemble the cleaned reads using SPAdes v3
steps.instruction: Assemble the cleaned reads using SPAdes v3.13.0 with default settings and careful flags to reduce mismatches: https://github.com/ablab/spades .

steps.title: Perform annotation using Prokka v1
steps.instruction: Perform annotation using Prokka v1.12 and identify rRNA genes. Annotate the assembled genome using Prokka v1.12 for gene prediction and identification of rRNA, tRNA, and CDS: https://github.com/tseemann/prokka .

steps.title: Conduct functional annotation using the NCBI...
steps.instruction: Conduct functional annotation using the NCBI non-redundant protein database (BlastX) and KEGG database (KAAS). Do pathway mapping using KEGG Automatic Annotation Server: https://www.genome.jp/tools/kaas/ .

steps.title: Construct a circular genome using CGView server 1
steps.instruction: Construct a circular genome using CGView server 1.0 ( Figure 2 ). Figure

steps.title: Circular genomic map of Streptomyces sp
steps.instruction: Circular genomic map of Streptomyces sp. VITGV100 assembled contigs are depicted as green arrows, accompanied by positive and negative GC skews indicated in the inner circle

steps.title: All analyses were run in Ubuntu...
steps.instruction: All analyses were run in Ubuntu Linux, with Trimmomatic and SPAdes via command line, and Blast2GO on a Java-supported desktop client. The genome sequencing was done with Eurofins Genomics Pvt. Ltd, and results were compiled. C. Secondary metabolite gene cluster analysis

steps.title: Detect biosynthetic gene clusters (BGCs) using antiSMASH 6
steps.instruction: Detect biosynthetic gene clusters (BGCs) using antiSMASH 6.0 ( Figures 3 and 4 ).

steps.title: The biosynthetic potential of Streptomyces sp
steps.instruction: The biosynthetic potential of Streptomyces sp. VITGV100 was analyzed using antiSMASH 6.0, which identified multiple secondary metabolite biosynthetic gene clusters, as summarized in Table 1 .

steps.title: Secondary metabolite cluster of Streptomyces sp
steps.instruction: Secondary metabolite cluster of Streptomyces sp. T1PKS: Type 1 Polyketide synthases. T2PKS: Type 2 Polyketide synthases. T3PKS: Type 3 Polyketide synthases. RiPP: Ribosomally synthesized and post-translationally modified peptides. Figure

steps.title: Screenshot highlighting two major stions
steps.instruction: Screenshot highlighting two major stions: core gene table prioritization and visualization of the BGC of indole located in scaffold 37.1 region of the genome Figure

steps.title: Screenshot highlighting two major stions
steps.instruction: Screenshot highlighting two major stions: core gene table prioritization and visualization of BGC of indole located in scaffold 70.1 region of the genome D. Gene ontology analysis

steps.title: Perform GO annotations using Blast2GO platform
steps.instruction: Perform GO annotations using Blast2GO platform. Gene ontology enrichment visualization: http://wego.genomics.org.cn/ .

steps.title: Categorize gene functions into biological process...
steps.instruction: Categorize gene functions into biological process (BP), molecular function (MF), and cellular component (CC).

steps.title: Retrieve GO terms using WEGO portal and Blast2GO
steps.instruction: Retrieve GO terms using WEGO portal and Blast2GO. E. Metabolite extraction

steps.title: Grow subcultures in ISP2 broth at...
steps.instruction: Grow subcultures in ISP2 broth at 30 °C for 15 days with a 1:50 inoculum ratio and shaking at 120 rpm.

steps.title: Confirm cellular growth by visual inspection...
steps.instruction: Confirm cellular growth by visual inspection of pellets and aggregates ( Figure 5 ). Figure

steps.title: Visual inspection of pellets and aggregates of Streptomyces sp
steps.instruction: Visual inspection of pellets and aggregates of Streptomyces sp. VITGV100

steps.title: Centrifuge the culture broth at 100...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the culture broth at 100 × g for 20 min at 4 °C and collect the supernatant in a separate tube. Discard cells.

steps.title: To the supernatant, add an equal...
steps.instruction: To the supernatant, add an equal volume of ethyl acetate and shake the mixture vigorously for 10 min.

steps.title: Incubate the flask in a rotary...
steps.instruction: Incubate the flask in a rotary shaker at 200 rpm for 24 h at room temperature to enhance the extraction process. Bead beating is not required, but may enhance extraction efficiency.

steps.title: Allow the mixture to settle undisturbed...
steps.instruction: Allow the mixture to settle undisturbed for 30 min, enabling phase separation.

steps.title: Carefully collect the upper organic phase...
steps.instruction: Carefully collect the upper organic phase containing the metabolites and concentrate it using a rotary evaporator at 54 °C and 80 rpm until a dry or semi-solid residue is obtained. Collect organic extracts in sterile glass vials to avoid solvent interaction with plastic.. After drying the solvent in a rotary evaporator, weigh the resulting residue.

steps.title: Dissolve the dried crude extract in...
steps.instruction: Dissolve the dried crude extract in 200 μL of acetonitrile to ensure proper solubility and store it at -20 °C for further use.

steps.title: Blank media extract was included as...
steps.instruction: Blank media extract was included as an ISP2 control to validate compound specificity during GC–MS analysis, as shown in Figure 6 . Figure

steps.title: Blank media extract was run as...
steps.instruction: Blank media extract was run as an ISP2 control during GC–MS analysis F. GC–MS analysis

steps.title: Use a TG-5MS fused silica capillary...
steps.instruction: Use a TG-5MS fused silica capillary column (30 m × 0.25 mm × 0.1 mm film thickness).

steps.title: Set electron ionization at 70 eV...
steps.instruction: Set electron ionization at 70 eV and helium flow at 1 mL/min.

steps.title: Maintain the injector and MS transfer...
steps.instruction: Maintain the injector and MS transfer line temperature at 280 °C.

steps.title: Use the following temperature program
steps.instruction: Use the following temperature program: a. 50 °C (2 min hold). b. Increase to 150 °C at 7 °C/min. c. Increase to 270 °C at 5 °C /min. d. Increase to 310 °C at 3.5 °C/min. The major chemical constituents present in the crude extract of Streptomyces sp. VITGV100, as identified by GC–MS analysis, are listed in Table 2 . GC–MS analysis revealed that the ethyl acetate extract of Streptomyces sp. VITGV100 contained 35 metabolites. Indole was detected at 12.410 min. The identity of each compound was confirmed using the NIST mass spectrometry database. Figure 7 presents the GC–MS chromatogram obtained from the crude extract. Figure 8 shows the mass spectrum of indole obtained from the crude extract. Table

steps.title: Cut 6 mm diameter wells in...
steps.instruction: Cut 6 mm diameter wells in agar plates using a sterile steel cork borer.

steps.title: Compare the antibacterial activity of the...
steps.instruction: Compare the antibacterial activity of the crude extract with tetracycline (positive control).

steps.title: Prepare four different concentrations (25, 50,...
steps.instruction: Prepare four different concentrations (25, 50, 75, and 100 μg/mL) of the crude extract in ethyl acetate and add to 4 different wells (100 μL each). Include negative controls (ethyl acetate) and positive controls (tetracycline) in all experiments.

steps.title: evenly using a sterile cotton swab...
steps.instruction: evenly using a sterile cotton swab to create a confluent lawn. Air-dry plates for 10 min before well diffusion.

steps.title: Prepare each test plate with a...
steps.instruction: Prepare each test plate with a uniform lawn of the respective test bacteria, including Escherichia coli and Staphylococcus aureus , to evaluate the antibacterial activity of the crude extract.

steps.title: Incubate plates at 37 °C for 24 h
steps.instruction: Incubate plates at 37 °C for 24 h.

steps.title: The antimicrobial activity of the crude...
steps.instruction: The antimicrobial activity of the crude extract was evaluated against selected test organisms ( Figure 9 ), and the results, expressed as mean inhibition zone diameters (± SD, n = 3), are presented in Table 3 . Figure

description: Validation of protocol This protocol has been rigorously tested to ensure reproducibility and robustness across multiple independent experiments. 1. Experimental validation All experiments were performed with a minimum of three biological replicates and three technical replicates per sample. Negative controls (untreated samples) and positive controls (reference standards) were included in all experiments. 2. Reproducibility and consistency Independent validation experiments were conducted under identical conditions, yielding consistent colony morphology, metabolite production, and genome sequencing results. Whole-genome sequencing quality metrics (e.g., N50 values, read coverage, and BUSCO completeness scores) confirmed the reliability of the sequencing and assembly steps. Metabolite profiling via GC–MS demonstrated a high degree of reproducibility, with consistent compound retention times and mass spectra across replicates.
